emergent_themes.description: Students living with parents who handle groceries/cooking but maintain personal food delivery autonomy and spending
How cashback mechanisms create personal benefits when ordering for groups, encouraging the subscriber to be the group orderer
Physical campus marketing and voucher distribution as particularly effective student engagement tactics
How having a subscription creates pressure to order more frequently to justify the cost

coding_summary: P009 reveals a sophisticated student delivery user who has evolved from occasional to strategic frequent ordering (2-3x/week) driven by academic/work schedule intensification. Living with parents creates unique dynamics where family handles groceries but he maintains £50-75/week personal food spending autonomy. His dual-subscription strategy (Uber One + Deliveroo student) demonstrates platform arbitrage behavior, with clear switching thresholds (£3-4 price difference). Cashback mechanics drive both individual loyalty and group ordering leadership. Campus marketing emerges as highly effective channel. Strong future subscription retention indicated despite losing student pricing.
